creator: pavel.plesov@gmail.com
text:
Hello,

I have just did the following:
1. Open http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=3Dports/132120 in my
firefox 3.0.5
2. Copy content using mouse selection from my last reply at Mon, 2 Mar
2009 14:08:47 +0300
=A0 (have no idea why I cannot use download link there)
3. Run vim inside gnome-terminal
4. Inside vim run: :set nopaste
5. Copy content from clipboard by middle click into vim
6. Save patch file as /tmp/1.patch
7. Restore original Makefile for xmobar
8. Run patch as following:
[root /usr/ports/x11/xmobar]# patch < /tmp/1.patch
Hmm... =A0Looks like a unified diff to me...
The text leading up to this was:
--------------------------
|--- /usr/ports/x11/xmobar/Makefile.orig =A0 =A0 =A0 =A02009-02-26
12:55:40.000000000 +0300
|+++ /usr/ports/x11/xmobar/Makefile =A0 =A0 2009-03-02 11:27:25.000000000 +=
0300
--------------------------
Patching file Makefile using Plan A...
Hunk #1 succeeded at 6.
Hunk #2 succeeded at 27.
Hunk #3 succeeded at 51.
Hmm... =A0Ignoring the trailing garbage.
done

Failures: 0 successes: 3

9. Run portlint as following:
[root /usr/ports/x11/xmobar]# portlint -V
portlint version 2.10.2

[root /usr/ports/x11/xmobar]# portlint
WARN: Makefile: [52]: possible direct use of command "install" found.
use ${INSTALL_foobaa} instead.
0 fatal errors and 1 warning found.

Patch version is:
[root /usr/ports/x11/xmobar]# patch -v
Patch version 2.1

What I am doing wrong ?


On Tue, Mar 10, 2009 at 8:45 AM, Gabor PALI <pgj@freebsd.org> wrote:
> For your information, portlint still gives the following errors and
> warnings (for the latest patch):
>
> FATAL: Makefile: [30]: use a tab (not space) after a variable name
> FATAL: Makefile: [35]: use a tab (not space) after a variable name
> WARN: Makefile: [38]: use a tab (not space) after a variable name
> WARN: Makefile: [39]: use a tab (not space) after a variable name
> WARN: Makefile: [41]: use a tab (not space) after a variable name
> WARN: Makefile: [42]: use a tab (not space) after a variable name
>
>
> Cheers,
> :g
>



--
Pavel Plesov
